2026 D3 WBB NCAA Tournament Bracket Analysis

Sectional hosting, flight projections, win probabilities & path difficulty

The NCAA D3 tournament places 64 teams into 4 quadrants of 16. After two rounds, each quadrant produces 4 Sweet 16 teams that play at a single sectional host site. The NCAA prefers the highest seed to host, but the 500-mile rule means teams 500+ miles away require a flight (paid by NCAA). To minimize flights, a lower seed may host if it keeps more teams within driving distance. This report enumerates all 256 possible 4-team sectionals per quadrant, calculates their probability using efficiency-based win models, and determines the likely host and flight count for each scenario.
Overall Summary Path Analysis Path Difficulty Bracket Map Top LeftTop RightBottom RightBottom Left

Win Probability & Path Analysis

Probability of each team reaching every tournament round, calculated by propagating efficiency-based win probabilities through the full bracket tree. All games assumed neutral-site. R32 = Round of 32, S16 = Sweet 16, E8 = Elite 8, FF = Final Four, Champ = Championship Game, Win = Win Title.

Easiest Path (1-seeds)
Scranton
Bottom Left — 51.9% Win
Hardest Path (1-seeds)

Top Right — 3.7% Win

Opponent-Based Path Difficulty

How tough is each team's bracket draw, independent of the team itself? For each round, this shows the expected opponent Adjusted Efficiency Margin (adjEM), weighted by the probability each potential opponent reaches that round. Higher EM = tougher expected opponent. The Total sums all six rounds — a team with a high total faces the hardest gauntlet regardless of its own strength. Unlike Win%, which rewards being dominant, this metric purely measures the opponents in your path.

Hardest Path (Overall)
(12) Union (NY)
Bottom Left — Total: +267.2
